title: Signals and Systems
summary: This page contains problems related to complex signals, system classifications, and system linearity. Problems cover plotting complex signals with various symmetry properties (even, odd, conjugate symmetric, conjugate antisymmetric), identifying SISO/MISO/SIMO/MIMO systems from personal experience, and determining linearity or nonlinearity of systems described by differential equations.

figures.caption: A mass M subjected to a horizontal input force x(t) whose resulting horizontal displacement is the output y(t).
figures.description: The figure shows a single mass labeled M above a horizontal reference line, with a right-pointing input force arrow labeled x(t) acting on the mass. A dotted vertical line projects the mass position to the baseline, where the output displacement is labeled y(t), and the positive horizontal axis is marked y pointing to the right. Students should notice the input-output relationship of a SISO translational system: applied horizontal force x(t) produces horizontal displacement y(t) of the mass.
